q: The electrolytes usually used in the electroplating of gold and silver, respectively, are:
question: The electrolytes usually used in the electroplating of gold and silver, respectively, are:
solution:
The solution of dicyano complexes of gold (Au) and silver (Ag) are used as electrolytes while performing electroplating with them.

The amine complexes of gold are not stable, so, they cannot be used.

Silver cyanide is employed as cyanide reacts with silver to form stable complexes.

options: At <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mo><</mo><mn>1200</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math>, oxidation of carbon is unfavourable
Oxidation of carbon is favourable at all temperatures

At <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mo><</mo><mn>1200</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math>, the reaction <math><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is spontaneous

At <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mo>></mo><mn>1200</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math>, carbon will reduce <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>MO</mi><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mfenced></msub><mo> </mo><mi>to</mi><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mfenced></msub></math>.
solution:
ΔG=Δ G 0 +RTlnQ

At Equilibrium ΔG=0  & Q=K

∆Go=-RTln⁡Kp

∴-ln⁡Kp=∆GoRT

∆Go∝ Temperature. At high temperature ∆Go value is very high so non-feasible or non-spontaneous process takes place.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
q: Which one of the following reactions does not occur during extraction of copper ?
question: Which one of the following reactions does not occur during extraction of copper ?
solution:
The following changes occur during smelting.

(a) Most of the ferrous sulphide gets oxidised to ferrous oxide which combines with silica (flux) to form fusible slag.

2FeS + 3O2 → 2FeO + 2SO2

FeO + SiO2 → FeSiO3 (Ferrous silicate)

The slag being lighter floats and forms the upper layer. It is removed through the slag hole from time to time.

(b) During roasting or in the blast furnace if any oxide of copper is formed, it combines with FeS and is changed back into its sulphide.

2Cu2S + 3O2 → 2Cu2O + 2SO2

Cu2O + FeS → Cu2S + FeO

Ferrous oxide thus formed again combines with silica to form more slag.

FeO+SiO2→FeSiO3

So, no formation of calcium silicate CaSiO3 in extraction of Cu.

q: Which of the following is used as a stabilizer during the concentration of sulphide ores?
question: Which of the following is used as a stabilizer during the concentration of sulphide ores?
solution: Froth flotation is a process for selectively separating hydrophobic materials from hydrophilic. The principle of froth flotation process is that sulphide ores are preferentially wetted by the pine oil, whereas the gangue particles are wetted by the water. Cresols and aniline are used as froth stabilisers.

q: In the electrolysis of alumina to obtain aluminium metal, cryolite is added mainly to
question: In the electrolysis of alumina to obtain aluminium metal, cryolite is added mainly to
options: lower the melting point of alumina
solution: Fused alumina $\left(\mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3\right)$ is a bad conductor of electricity. Therefore, cryolite $\left(\mathrm{Na}_3 \mathrm{AlF}_6\right)$ and fluorspar $\left(\mathrm{CaF}_2\right)$ are added to purified alumina which not only make alumina a good conductor of electricity but also reduce the melting point of the mixture to around $1140 \mathrm{~K}$.

q:
Assertion: For the extraction of iron, haematite ore is used.
Reason: Haematite is a carbonate ore of iron.
question:
Assertion: For the extraction of iron, haematite ore is used.
Reason: Haematite is a carbonate ore of iron.
options: Both the assertion and reason are correct and the reason is the correct explanation for the assertion.
Both the assertion and reason are correct, but the reason is not the correct explanation for the assertion.
solution:
Iron can be extracted from Haematite ore. The Haematite is the mineral form of iron (III) oxide, one of several iron oxides.
Haematite is not a carbonate ore. Hence, the reason is false.

<math><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mo>)</mo></math>

<math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>

<math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>-</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mo>)</mo></math>
solution:
Liquation is used to purify the metal whose melting point is lower than that of impurities like Sn, Bi,Pb etc.
Mond’s process The impure nickle is heated in presence of carbon monoxide. 

Nis + COg →NiCO4g → Nis + 4COg
Zone refining is based on the principle that impurities are more soluble in melt that in the pure solid metal. The metals like Ga, Ge etc. are purified by this method.
Van Arkell method  is used for those metals which are very difficult to obtain in pure state like Zr.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
q: In liquation process used for tin <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>Sn</mi></mfenced></math>, the metal
question: In liquation process used for tin <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>Sn</mi></mfenced></math>, the metal
options: is brought to molten form which is made to flow on a slope
solution:
Liquation is a metallurgical method for separating metals from an ore or alloy.

This method was largely used to remove lead containing silver from copper, but it can also be used to remove antimony minerals from ore, and refine tin.

Liquation process is used for metal having low melting point such as tin in which they are heated and brought to molten state and made to flow down the slope while impurities with higher melting point left on the top.

Liquation is similar to distillation, except instead of vaporizing the impure metal, it is melted.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
q: The Mond process is used for the:
question: The Mond process is used for the:
options: Purification of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi></math>
solution:
Mond's Process: In this process, nickel is heated in a stream of carbon monoxide forming a volatile complex, nickel tetracarbonyl:

Nis+4COg→330-350 KNiCO4g

The carbonyl is subjected to a higher temperature so that it is decomposed giving the pure metal:

NiCO4 →450-470 KNi+4CO

q: Ellingham diagram is a graphical representation of :
question: Ellingham diagram is a graphical representation of :
solution:
Ellingham diagram is a graphical representation of ΔG vs T when metal heated with oxygen to form metal oxide.

An Ellingham diagram is a graph showing the temperature dependence of the stability for compounds. This analysis is usually used to evaluate the ease of reduction of metal oxides and sulphides.

Ellingham diagram represents change in free energy ΔG with temperature

Hall Heroult's process:

At cathode: Al3++3e⊝→Al

At anode: O2-+C→CO+2e⊝

Overall reaction: 2Al2O3+3C→4Al+3CO2

question:
Among following compounds, the number of those present in copper matte is

A. CuCO3

B. Cu2S

C. Cu2O

D. FeO
solution: Copper Matte is a combination of copper sulphide and a small amount of iron sulphide. In the process of copper extraction, Matte is the stage after the final reduction process which involves converting it to crude copper.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
q:
The methods NOT involved in concentration of ore are

(A) Liquation
(B) Leaching
(C) Electrolysis
(D) Hydraulic washing
(E) Froth floatation

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
The methods NOT involved in concentration of ore are

(A) Liquation
(B) Leaching
(C) Electrolysis
(D) Hydraulic washing
(E) Froth floatation

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
Electrolysis is not a concentration method. Electrolytic refining is a process of refining a metal (mainly copper) by the process of electrolysis.

Liquation is not a concentration method. It is a refining method. In this method a low melting metal like tin can be made to flow on a sloping surface. In this way it is separated from higher melting impurities.

The other three methods are concentration methods of ores.

Leaching is used when the ore is soluble in a solvent. The powdered ore is dissolved in a chemical, usually a strong solution of NaOH. The chemical solution dissolves the metal in the ore, and it can be extracted and separated from the gangue by extracting the chemical solution.

Froth flotation is a process for selectively separating hydrophobic materials from hydrophilic.

Hydraulic washing is a technique used when the impurities are lighter and the ore particles are heavier. The lighter impurities are removed by washing in current of water. As gold particles are heavier than the impurities like sand, we can use hydraulic washing for the concentration of ores of gold.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
q: Which of the reaction is suitable for concentrating ore by leaching process?
question: Which of the reaction is suitable for concentrating ore by leaching process?
solution:
Leaching: It involves the treatment of the ore with a suitable reagent as to make it soluble while impurity remains insoluble and after this, the ore is recovered from the solution by suitable methods.

The principal ore of aluminium and bauxite usually contain SiO2, iron oxides and titanium oxides as impurities.

Al2O3 s+2NaOH aq+3H2O→2NaAlOH4 aq2NaAlOH4 aq+CO2 g→Al2O3·xH2O s+2NaHCO3 aq

The sodium silicate remains in the solution and hydrated alumina is filtered, dried and heated to give pure Al2O3.

Al2O3·xH2O s→1470 KAl2O3 s+xH2O g

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
q: Which one of the following is most likely a mismatch?
question: Which one of the following is most likely a mismatch?
solution:
Mond's process involves the combination of carbon monoxide with nickel readily and reversibly and gives nickel tetracarbonyl. This process is used to extract and purify nickel.

The Van Arkel technique is used to refine Zirconium or Titanium. Impurities found in Zirconium or Titanium in the form of nitrogen and oxygen are eliminated using this technique.

Copper is purified by electrolysis. Electricity is passed through solutions containing copper compounds, such as copper sulphate. The anode (positive electrode ) is made from impure copper and the cathode (negative electrode) is made from pure copper.

Mercury and zinc are purified by distillation method. Liquation is a metallurgical method to remove lead containing silver from copper and remove antimony minerals from ore.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
q:
Among statements (a) – (d), the correct ones are :
(a) Lime stone is decomposed to CaO during the extraction of iron from its oxides.
(b) In the extraction of silver, silver is extracted as an anionic complex.
(c) Nickel is purified by Mond’s process.
(d) Zr and Ti are purified by Van Arkel method.
question:
Among statements (a) – (d), the correct ones are :
(a) Lime stone is decomposed to CaO during the extraction of iron from its oxides.
(b) In the extraction of silver, silver is extracted as an anionic complex.
(c) Nickel is purified by Mond’s process.
(d) Zr and Ti are purified by Van Arkel method.
solution:
(a) During the extraction of Iron. at 900–1500 K (higher temperature range in the blast furnace), one of the processes taking place is:

CaCO3→∆CaO+CO2

Limestone is decomposed to CaO which removes silicate impurity of the ore as slag. The slag is in the molten state and separates out from iron.

(b) Extraction of silver involves leaching the metal with cyanide ion:

4Ags+8CN–aq+2H2Oaq+O2g→4AgCN2–aq+4OH–aq

The complex formed is negatively charged and thus it is an anionic complex.

(c) Nickel is purified by Mond's process:

Step-1: Nickel is heated in a stream of carbon monoxide forming a volatile complex, nickel tetracarbonyl.

Ni+4CO→330-350 KNiCO4

Step-2: The carbonyl is subjected to a higher temperature so that it is decomposed giving the pure metal.

NiCO4→450-470 KNi+4CO

(d) van Arkel Method for Refining Zirconium or Titanium:

This method is very useful for removing all the oxygen and nitrogen present in the form of impurity in certain metals like Zr and Ti. The crude metal is heated in an evacuated vessel with iodine. The metal iodide being more covalent, volatilises.

Zr+2I2→ZrI4

The metal iodide is decomposed on a tungsten filament, electrically heated to about 1800 K. The pure metal is thus deposited on the filament.

ZrI4→Zr+2I2

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
q:
Match List-I with List-II

List-I List-II
(a) Sodium Carbonate (i) Deacon
(b) Titanium (ii) Castner-Kellner
(c) Chlorine (iii) van-Arkel
(d) Sodium hydroxide (iv) Solvay

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Match List-I with List-II

List-I List-II
(a) Sodium Carbonate (i) Deacon
(b) Titanium (ii) Castner-Kellner
(c) Chlorine (iii) van-Arkel
(d) Sodium hydroxide (iv) Solvay

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
(a) Sodium carbonate Na2CO3 & NaHCO3 are prepared by Solvay process.

(b) Titanium is refined by Van-Arkel process.

Titanium : Van arkel method

Ti+I2⟶T1TiI4g

TiI4g→T2>T1T2TiRefined titanium+2I2g

(c) Chlorine is prepared by Deacon process.
